2014-04-01.title: Climate Study Puts Diplomatic Pressure on Obama
2014-04-01.abstract: With no chance that Congress will agree to a huge increase in so-called climate aid, a new study on the effects of climate change creates a diplomatic challenge for President Obama.

2014-04-01.title: Behind Barbed Wire, Shakespeare Inspires a Cast of Young Syrians
2014-04-01.abstract: Syrian children at a refugee camp in Jordan performed Shakespeare’s “King Lear,” a tragedy that briefly took their minds off their own miseries.

2014-04-01.title: Japan: Man Freed From Death Row Faces New Effort to Block a Retrial
2014-04-01.abstract: Prosecutors filed an appeal on Monday to block a court from granting a retrial to a former boxer convicted of murder whose release last week after 48 years on death row exposed problems in Japan’s justice system.

2014-04-01.title: Years After Obama Hailed Warming Ties With India, the Temperature Has Fallen
2014-04-01.abstract: The once warm relationship between India and the United States has decidedly cooled, marked by disagreements over important diplomatic issues, like the crisis in Ukraine.

2014-04-01.title: Years After Revolt, Chinese Village Glumly Returns to Polls
2014-04-01.abstract: Despite an uprising and democratic elections years ago, many voters said they had come to view their earlier electoral success as the beginning of the end of local self-governance.

2014-04-02.title: Authorities Kill Gang Leader, Mexico Says
2014-04-02.abstract: Mexican authorities said they had killed another leader of the Knights Templar drug gang, adding to a string of successes against the organization that seemed to leave only one boss still at large.

2014-04-02.title: To Protect Foreigners, Afghanistan Shuts Down Their Hangouts
2014-04-02.abstract: After a wave of violence in the Afghan capital, several restaurants and guesthouses popular with foreigners were ordered closed until after elections on Saturday.

2014-04-02.title: Afghan Women See Hope in the Ballot Box
2014-04-02.abstract: There’s finally a sense that women have become a significant part of Afghanistan’s political life, but many fear the gains could be reversed if extremists retake power or Western aid dwindles.

2014-04-02.title: House Approves Funds for Ukraine
2014-04-02.abstract: President Obama said he would sign the bill providing for loan guarantees and sanctions against Russia.

2014-04-02.title: Earthquake Hits Off Coast of North Chile
2014-04-02.abstract: A strong quake struck off Chile, forcing an evacuation along the country’s long coastline and putting the entire Pacific Coast of South America on tsunami alerts.

2014-04-03.title: Iran’s Choice for U.N. Post Fuels Tension With U.S.
2014-04-03.abstract: Washington has yet to announce a decision on a visa for Hamid Aboutalebi, a diplomat with links to the group behind the 1979 hostage crisis in Tehran.

2014-04-03.title: War and Unrest Provide for a Scarred Campaign Trail in Afghanistan
2014-04-03.abstract: Amid repeated Taliban attacks and a continuing war, Afghan presidential candidates step onto planes with guards carrying AK-47s, and rocket-propelled grenades are sometimes part of carry-on baggage.

2014-04-03.title: Campaign of Resilience
2014-04-03.abstract: Afghans voted in the first election in the nation’s modern history with the potential to bring a peaceful change of leadership.

2014-04-03.title: Afghan Candidates Make Final Pitches
2014-04-03.abstract: Before Saturday’s presidential election, Afghan candidates campaigned under tight security amid worries of violence and voter fraud.

2014-04-03.title: NASA Breaks Most Contact With Russia
2014-04-03.abstract: The space agency suspended contact except over operations of the International Space Station, underscoring the rapid deterioration of ties in the wake of the Kremlin’s annexation of Crimea.

2014-04-04.title: China: U.S. to Shun Joint Naval Drills
2014-04-04.abstract: The United States will not send a fleet to take part in naval exercises that China is hosting this month, military officials said Thursday.

2014-04-04.title: Iraqi Envoy Calls on U.S. to Strengthen Relationship
2014-04-04.abstract: The ambassador, Lukman Faily, said that the United States should be more generous in providing assistance but emphasized he did not advocate a resumed military presence.

2014-04-04.title: Karzai Is Trying to Keep His Sway After Term Ends
2014-04-04.abstract: As Afghanistan girds for its presidential election, an exercise that President Hamid Karzai has shaped at every stage, those hoping to see him retire quietly may be disappointed.

2014-04-04.title: Israel: Airstrikes in Gaza Follow Militants’ Rocket Attacks
2014-04-04.abstract: Israeli military aircraft carried out multiple airstrikes in the Gaza Strip early Friday, the Israeli military and Palestinian officials said, after militants fired four rockets into southern Israel late Thursday.

2014-04-04.title: U.S. Says It Tried to Build a Social Media Site in Cuba, but Failed
2014-04-04.abstract: The Obama administration insists that the effort to create a Twitter-like network was intended to encourage political discussion, not to foment rebellion.

2014-04-05.title: Lingering Hope vs. Onset of Grief Over Lost Flight
2014-04-05.abstract: Two sons of passengers on Flight 370 have chosen different ways to cope: One holds vigil with other passengers’ relatives, while the other is assuming the worst.

2014-04-05.title: U.S. Eyes Afghan Vote, Seeking Amenable Ally
2014-04-05.abstract: Obama administration officials will closely watch this weekend’s election in Afghanistan, hoping that a successor to President Hamid Karzai will allow international troops to remain on the ground.

2014-04-05.title: Curtis Bill Pepper, Author, Reporter and Traveler, Is Dead at 96
2014-04-05.abstract: Mr. Pepper, a prolific journalist and author, excelled at finding anecdotes that revealed greater truths about his subjects.

2014-04-05.title: French Push U.N. to Seek War Crimes Case in Syria
2014-04-05.abstract: The French have taken the first steps toward a Security Council referral of Syrians suspected of war crimes to the International Criminal Court.

2014-04-05.title: We Were Just Laughing: Days With Anja Niedringhaus and Kathy Gannon
2014-04-05.abstract: A reporter who was recently with Anja Niedringhaus and Kathy Gannon of The Associated Press, longtime fellow chroniclers of events in Afghanistan, reflects on the attack on them and the changing environment in the country.

2014-04-06.title: Delays in Effort to Refocus C.I.A. From Drone War
2014-04-06.abstract: Despite avowals about the need to refocus the agency on its original missions of analysis, intelligence collecting and espionage, the paramilitary operations have proven hard to give up.

2014-04-06.title: A Change in Atmosphere in Central Syria
2014-04-06.abstract: The boom of shelling no longer dominates the days and nights.

2014-04-06.title: Relative Calm in Parts of Syria Is Deceptive
2014-04-06.abstract: Beneath a calm imposed by military force, siege and starvation, the stage appears set for an unstable period of prolonged conflict that could explode again in months or years.

2014-04-06.title: Citizenship-for-Cash Program in Malta Stirs Security Concerns in European Union
2014-04-06.abstract: Opponents said the program, under which foreigners can pay to buy Maltese citizenship, could open the door to the West for criminals or terrorists.

2014-04-06.title: Election in Hungary Tests Nation’s Tilt to the Right
2014-04-06.abstract: Prime Minister Viktor Orban and his coalition appear headed for victory Sunday, but the election will gauge the depth of voters’ support and their interest in pushing Hungary further right.

2014-04-07.title: State Visit of Ireland’s President to Britain, a First, Underlines Evolution of Relations
2014-04-07.abstract: When Michael D. Higgins goes to Britain on Monday for a state visit, the first for an Irish president, the theme of reconciliation between two nations will be on display.

2014-04-07.title: Fashion District Robberies Turn Heads, and Some Can’t Help Smirking
2014-04-07.abstract: A series of brazen smash-and-grab thefts at expensive shops in the Vendôme district has been cause for both alarm and satire.

2014-04-07.title: Business Titans Too Rich to Bribe Seek to Ease Fears in East Ukraine
2014-04-07.abstract: Kiev is hoping that naming wealthy businessmen to positions of power will help allay the fears of Russian speakers in the east of the country.

2014-04-07.title: U.S. Tries Candor to Assure China on Cyberattacks
2014-04-07.abstract: The U.S. held an unusual briefing for China on its cyberwarfare strategy in an effort to establish “an understanding of rules of the road.” So far, the Chinese have not reciprocated.

2014-04-07.title: Early Tallies Indicating Afghan Vote a Success
2014-04-07.abstract: Election officials said the vote count indicated that about 60 percent of the 12 million eligible voters had cast ballots.

2014-04-08.title: Prisoner in Israel Is Linked to Talks
2014-04-08.abstract: Marwan Barghouti, a popular political figure and a convicted murderer serving multiple life sentences in Israel, could be an impetus to continue negotiations.

2014-04-08.title: Officials on Unrest in Ukraine
2014-04-08.abstract: On Monday, officials from the United States, Russia and Britain discussed the growing unrest and violence in the Ukrainian cities of Donetsk, Kharkiv and Luhansk.

2014-04-08.title: Concession Offered, Taiwan Group to End Protest of China Trade Pact
2014-04-08.abstract: Activists, mostly students, who stormed Taiwan’s legislature to show resistance to a trade deal with China, agreed on Monday to leave the building later this week.

2014-04-08.title: Iran’s Choice for U.N. Post Denied Entry Into the U.S.
2014-04-08.abstract: Hamid Aboutalebi, who was to become Iran’s ambassador to the United Nations in July, was a member of the Muslim student group that took American hostages in 1979.

2014-04-08.title: In Switch, Development Agency Welcomes Business and Technology to Poverty Fight
2014-04-08.abstract: Rajiv Shah, head of the United States Agency for International Development, has set off a fierce debate over the way the agency operates and the usefulness of traditional foreign aid.

2014-04-09.title: Search for Malaysian Jet to Be Costliest in History
2014-04-09.abstract: Many of the governments involved will face a difficult decision about whether to keep bearing the extraordinary expense.

2014-04-09.title: Rounding Up Suspects, Pakistan Charges a Baby
2014-04-09.abstract: The country’s troubled criminal judicial system has not been hesitant with Musa Khan, a 9-month-old boy who faces charges of attempted murder.

2014-04-09.title: Parti Québécois Failed to Renew Mandate, Analysts Say
2014-04-09.abstract: “People are fed up” with the debate over whether Quebec should separate from Canada, one commentator wrote.

2014-04-09.title: Red Herrings and Overlooked Clues in Plane Search
2014-04-09.abstract: Missteps, false leads and bureaucratic delays have thwarted the search for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370.

2014-04-09.title: Updated Q. and A. on Malaysia Airlines Flight 370
2014-04-09.abstract: Tracing the mysterious pings, and other points of interest.

2014-04-10.title: In a Land of On-Time Arrivals, a Tour of Anything But
2014-04-10.abstract: Berlin’s new airport is attracting tourists after a series of delays and bungles have driven its cost billions of dollars over budget and pushed its opening back indefinitely.

2014-04-10.title: U.S. Pair Can’t Leave Qatar Amid Appeal
2014-04-10.abstract: Sentenced to three years in their daughter’s death, Matthew and Grace Huang of Los Angeles have been denied permission to travel.

2014-04-10.title: Mechanics of the Plane Search, Explained
2014-04-10.abstract: How the multinational team off the coast of Australia is continuing the search for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370.

2014-04-10.title: After Optimism of More Pings, Reality of Long Jet Search Settles In
2014-04-10.abstract: Specialists in ocean exploration and recovery cautioned that a successful identification of Malaysia Airlines Flight 370’s wreckage might take weeks or months.

2014-04-10.title: Syria: Deadly Car Bomb Blasts Hit Homs
2014-04-10.abstract: Two car bombs exploded minutes apart near a shop in the central Syrian city of Homs on Wednesday, killing at least 25 people, state news media and an antigovernment monitoring group reported.

2014-04-11.title: During Hagel Visit, China Showed Its Military Might, and Its Frustrations
2014-04-11.abstract: In his official visit to China, Defense Secretary Chuck Hagel encountered combative public warnings and private complaints that Beijing felt besieged by hostile neighbors.

2014-04-11.title: Russia: Lawmakers Want to Investigate Gorbachev
2014-04-11.abstract: Five members of the Russian Parliament have asked the prosecutor general to open a criminal case to investigate the breakup of the Soviet Union and the role played by Mikhail S. Gorbachev.

2014-04-11.title: Israel to Halt Tax Transfers to Palestinians
2014-04-11.abstract: The move came in response to the Palestinian leadership’s application last week to join 15 international conventions and treaties.

2014-04-11.title: Satellites Show Russian Forces Poised Near Ukraine
2014-04-11.abstract: A top NATO commander said the satellite photographs showed a menacing force of Russian fighter jets and tanks, along with as many as 40,000 troops, near the border with Ukraine.

2014-04-11.title: Mexico: Convicted Trafficker Now Aiding U.S. Officials
2014-04-11.abstract: The United States attorney in Chicago said that a top member of the Sinaloa gang had been cooperating with the authorities since he pleaded guilty to drug trafficking charges a year ago.

2014-04-12.title: Mystery Surrounds Death of Ukrainian Activist
2014-04-12.abstract: Oleksandr Muzychko’s gun-waving tactics and anger, largely directed against Russia, won him both admirers and enemies.

2014-04-12.title: Pope Francis Asks for Forgiveness
2014-04-12.abstract: The pope personally asked for forgiveness for the damage that priests have done “for having sexually abused children.”

2014-04-12.title: Pope Takes Responsibility in Priests’ Abuse Scandal
2014-04-12.abstract: Victims’ advocates complained that the unprecedented public statement of papal contrition over the scandal is still just “talk.”

2014-04-12.title: Japan: New Energy Strategy Approved
2014-04-12.abstract: Japan’s cabinet approved a new national energy strategy on Friday that designates nuclear power as an important energy source and calls for restarting idled nuclear plants that meet new safety standards.

2014-04-12.title: Police Drop Charges Against Baby in Pakistan
2014-04-12.abstract: The attempted murder charge against 9-month-old Musa Khan prompted widespread scorn of the Pakistani legal system.

2014-04-13.title: Lingering Power of Hostage Crisis Short-Circuits Iranian Nominee
2014-04-13.abstract: Washington’s decision to deny Hamid Aboutalebi, Iran’s choice for its next United Nations ambassador, a visa highlights the distrust that still infects the United States-Iran relationship.

2014-04-13.title: Thailand’s Political Tensions Are Rekindling Ethnic and Regional Divisions
2014-04-13.abstract: Three governments have been removed from power since 2006, and for the last five months, protesters in Bangkok have demanded the overthrow of the government and a reduction in the influence of the Shinawatra clan.

2014-04-13.title: Damascus and Rebels Trade Blame in Gas Attack
2014-04-13.abstract: The attack on a rebel-held Syrian village reportedly sickened more than 100 people.

2014-04-13.title: Xenophobic Chill Descends on Moscow
2014-04-13.abstract: From the moment that Russia’s invasion and annexation of Crimea cast a new, bitter chill over relations with the West, a sinister jingoistic vibe has pervaded Russia’s capital.

2014-04-14.title: Sanctions Are Eased; Iran Sees Little Relief
2014-04-14.abstract: Analysts say foreign business interest in Iran has remained extremely limited amid a temporary deal allowing increased economic ties.

2014-04-14.title: Beaming Good Cheer to a Norwegian Town’s Dark Days
2014-04-14.abstract: Three giant mirrors on a mountain reflect sunlight onto a small Norwegian town that gets very little of it in the winter. But not everyone is happy with the project.

2014-04-14.abstract: Despite evidence of frail health, Abdelaziz Bouteflika, 77, insisted on running again, a move that has united both secular and Islamic parties in their call to boycott the election.

2014-04-14.title: American’s Wish for Iranian Burial Riles Hard-Liners
2014-04-14.abstract: Politics intrudes after the death of Richard Nelson Frye, 94, a noted Harvard scholar who loved a land and its culture.

2014-04-14.title: Ron Pundak, a Key Israeli in Oslo Accords, Dies at 59
2014-04-14.abstract: Mr. Pundak was an academic enlisted to take part in the secret negotiations with Palestinian officials that led to a historic agreement.

2014-04-15.title: Immigrants Stir New Life Into São Paulo’s Gritty Old Center
2014-04-15.abstract: Entrepreneurs from Africa, Asia and Spanish-speaking Latin America are starting up businesses alongside the squalor and social ills of a downtrodden district.

2014-04-15.abstract: An Israeli police officer was fatally shot and his wife and young son were wounded as they drove to a Passover Seder near the West Bank city of Hebron.

2014-04-15.title: 2 Pakistanis Suspected of Cannibalism After Head Is Found
2014-04-15.abstract: The police arrested a man suspected of cannibalism after they found an infant’s head at his home, and they are searching for the man’s older brother.

2014-04-15.abstract: Iran on Monday said it had complained to the United Nations over the refusal by the United States to grant a visa to its choice for ambassador, Hamid Aboutalebi.

2014-04-15.title: Mexico: Homicides Decline Near Border, Report Says

2014-04-16.title: Milan Court Gives Berlusconi a Year of Community Service
2014-04-16.abstract: The former prime minister’s sentence, of four hours a week at a home for the elderly, was imposed as an alternative to a year in prison for tax fraud.

2014-04-16.abstract: The five-year economic collapse in Greece has spawned a new burst of creative energy that has turned Athens into a contemporary mecca for street art in Europe.

2014-04-16.title: Russia Is Quick to Bend Truth About Ukraine

2014-04-16.abstract: Gen. Wesley K. Clark and Philip A. Karber say that equipment like body armor and night goggles could help Ukraine face a Russian attack.

2014-04-16.title: Social Messages on Athenian Walls
2014-04-16.abstract: The anguish of the times has increasingly crept into the elaborate stencil work and multitude of large, colorful murals found all over the city.

2014-04-17.title: Between Obama and Putin, a Game of He Said, He Said
2014-04-17.abstract: U.S. and Russian summaries of a telephone conversation between Presidents Obama and Putin seemed to be talking about two different calls.

2014-04-17.title: Russia Economy Worsens Even Before Sanctions Hit
2014-04-17.abstract: With the country’s long-troubled economy teetering on the edge of recession, average Russians are feeling the pinch, even before the bite of Western sanctions over Crimea.

2014-04-17.abstract: The talks on Ukraine that are set to begin in Geneva on Thursday are a pivotal test of Russia’s intentions before the United States and European nations decide whether to impose more economic sanctions.

2014-04-17.title: Syrian War Takes Heavy Toll at a Crossroad of Cultures
2014-04-17.abstract: Fighting has damaged Palmyra and other archaeological sites, and made it easier for thieves to smuggle hundreds of antiquities out of the country.

2014-04-17.title: In Ukraine, Russia Plays a Weighted Word Game
2014-04-17.abstract: If one ignores the images of masked men armed to the teeth and just listens to Russian television, the drama in eastern Ukraine can seem like a quarrel among constitutional scholars.

2014-04-18.title: Time Is Short for High-Risk Rescue Effort
2014-04-18.abstract: Experts said that even if passengers aboard the capsized South Korean ferry found air pockets, time was short and the risk of hypothermia was high.

2014-04-18.title: Demands That Jews Register in Eastern Ukraine Are Denounced, and Denied
2014-04-18.abstract: The Donetsk People’s Republic, along with other pro-Russian groups, denied having anything to do with fliers purporting to demand registration.

2014-04-18.title: Stranded by the Fight Over a Border’s Future
2014-04-18.abstract: The influx of people into the Ras Khamis neighborhood of East Jerusalem is straining city services, and City Hall has shown little inclination to help.

2014-04-18.abstract: In a poke at the White House, Edward J. Snowden asked President Vladimir V. Putin about Russia’s surveillance practices during a televised question-and-answer session.

2014-04-18.abstract: The girls were kidnapped early Tuesday from a state school in the heartland of the Boko Haram insurgency, startling a country already numbed by a steady drumbeat of horrific episodes attributed to the Islamist militant group.

2014-04-19.title: Ukraine Seeks Inquiry Into Killings
2014-04-19.abstract: Ukraine has asked the International Criminal Court to investigate the deaths of civilians on its territory that occurred from Nov. 21, 2013, to Feb. 22, covering the period when more than 100 people were killed during antigovernment protests.

2014-04-19.title: Obama Signs Bill That Bars Iran’s Envoy
2014-04-19.abstract: President Obama signed a bill into law on Friday that would prohibit anyone who has engaged in espionage or terrorism against the United States from obtaining a visa to enter the country as a representative to the United Nations.

2014-04-19.title: Ex-Marine Held in Iran Asks Official for Help
2014-04-19.abstract: Amir Hekmati, an American incarcerated in Iran since August 2011, recently wrote to its foreign minister seeking help in gaining his release.

2014-04-19.title: Snowden Defends Query to Putin on Surveillance
2014-04-19.abstract: Edward J. Snowden insisted that he had been trying to hold Russia’s government to account over its surveillance practices, as he did with the Obama administration.

2014-04-19.title: Paper Shows U.S.-Flagged Plane in Iran Has Ties to Ghana
2014-04-19.abstract: The ownership ties between a Utah bank and a Ghanaian company raise questions about the role of sanctions.

2014-04-20.title: U.N. Struggles to Stem Haiti Cholera Epidemic
2014-04-20.abstract: The United Nations, facing a shortage of supplies to handle the epidemic, refuses to address whether its peacekeepers brought a deadly strain of the disease into the country.

2014-04-20.title: 1914: Easter Scenes In Paris
2014-04-20.abstract: During the Holy Week in Paris in 1914, streams of Parisians left the city for the Easter vacation, while tourists arrived to explore the capital.

2014-04-20.title: In Our Pages: April 21
2014-04-20.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: in 1914 Britain’s King George V and Queen Mary visit France in their first official visit since the coronation; in 1964 a massive power failure in London cancels the launch of the BBC 2 television channel.

2014-04-20.title: Clues to Family’s Business Ties Found in Public Paper Trail
2014-04-20.abstract: Documenting the Zhou family investment tree to assess the vast wealth accumulated by the family of the former national security chief, who also ran the giant China National Petroleum Corp.

2014-04-20.title: Fractured State of Pakistani Taliban Calls Peace Deal Into Question
2014-04-20.abstract: Infighting between commanders appears to be impeding talks with government negotiators, and recent bombings raise the possibility that the very idea of making peace has divided the Taliban.

2014-04-21.title: A Lead Grows, but Runoff Is Likely in Afghan Race
2014-04-21.abstract: The candidate Abdullah Abdullah widened his lead, but neither of the top contenders seemed to be on track to win more than 50 percent of the vote.

2014-04-21.title: Surviving the Mount Everest Avalanche
2014-04-21.abstract: Kaji Sherpa is a climber who survived the avalanche Friday on Mount Everest. From a hospital bed in Katmandu, he recounted the disaster that killed at least 13 Sherpas.

2014-04-21.title: After Everest Disaster, Sherpas Contemplate Strike
2014-04-21.abstract: As the Sherpas, who put themselves at great risk to guide affluent clients up Mount Everest, mulled an unprecedented strike, some would-be climbers said their passion for the ascent was gone.

2014-04-21.title: Errors Mounted as Chaos Ruled Capsizing Ferry
2014-04-21.abstract: A portrait of chaos is emerging from crew members, survivors and a transcript of the vessel’s final 40 minutes of communications with emergency dispatchers.

2014-04-21.title: A War Shrine Gesture in Japan Complicates Relations in Asia
2014-04-21.abstract: The prime minister, Shinzo Abe, sent a traditional offering to a shrine honoring Japan’s war dead, a move that may anger South Korea and China.

2014-04-22.title: Iran Disputes Reports of Violent Crackdown in Prison’s Political Detainee Block
2014-04-22.abstract: News accounts of more than 30 detainees being injured or placed in solitary confinement set off a rare protest outside Parliament on Sunday, but officials deny the reports.

2014-04-22.title: Vietnam Returns Migrants to China After Deadly Border Clash
2014-04-22.abstract: A photograph suggests the migrants may be part of the Uighur ethnic minority, whose members complain of oppression in China.

2014-04-22.title: Pakistan: Unicef Workers Freed
2014-04-22.abstract: Two Unicef staff members who were abducted last week in Pakistan’s southern port city of Karachi have been released.

2014-04-22.title: Investigated by China’s Leaders, Powerful Family Has a Defender: Its Hometown
2014-04-22.abstract: As China pursues a graft investigation against the family of Zhou Yongkang, many in its home village are quick to dispute reports.

2014-04-22.title: Under Russia, Life in Crimea Grows Chaotic
2014-04-22.abstract: A month after the lightning annexation of Crimea, residents find themselves in a state of confusion, with few institutions functioning normally, including banks, courts and foreign businesses.

2014-04-23.title: A.N.C.’s Stature Wanes as Disenchantment Grows in South Africa
2014-04-23.abstract: The venerated African National Congress is expected to win in the coming election, though unemployment and abuses of power are among the issues stirring opposition forces.

2014-04-23.title: Homs Emerges as Turning Point in Shaping Syria’s Future
2014-04-23.abstract: For both the government and opposition, the central Syrian crossroads city, practically reduced to rubble during the civil war, will play a crucial role in stitching the country back together.

2014-04-23.title: Sherpas Move to Shut Everest in Labor Fight
2014-04-23.abstract: Last week’s deadly avalanche has prompted Mount Everest’s Sherpas to threaten to cancel the season unless the government shares proceeds from what has become a multimillion-dollar industry.

2014-04-23.title: Korea Confronts Tendency to Overlook Safety as Toll in Ferry Sinking Grows
2014-04-23.abstract: Amid a list of errors that appeared to have contributed to the disaster, South Koreans are expressing shame and anger at how far their country still has to go to address inadequate safety measures and lax regulation.

2014-04-23.title: Qatar: Sentence of U.S. Couple Was Based on Lesser Charge
2014-04-23.abstract: A panel of judges that found an American couple guilty in the death of their adopted daughter convicted them on the charge of jeopardizing the child, and not the original charge of murder.

2014-04-24.title: Eastern Europe Frets About NATO’s Ability to Curb Russia
2014-04-24.abstract: NATO’s Eastern European members are growing increasingly nervous about Russia’s moves and the alliance’s ability, or even willingness, to counter them.

2014-04-24.title: Colombia’s President Reinstates a Mayor He Ousted
2014-04-24.abstract: President Juan Manuel Santos restored Gustavo Petro as the mayor of Bogotá a month after removing him from office to comply with a decision by the inspector general.

2014-04-24.title: Nepal Tries to Save Season as Everest Tourists Depart
2014-04-24.abstract: Tourism officials planned to visit the Mount Everest base camp Thursday to try to salvage the expedition season even as Sherpas and tour operators, shaken by last week’s avalanche deaths, began packing up and heading home.

2014-04-24.title: On Whiteboards, in Black Ink, Grim Descriptions of Those Lost as Ferry Sank
2014-04-24.abstract: The descriptions, some rendered in forensic detail, are the only evidence that many parents of the teenagers who died on South Korea’s doomed ferry have of their children’s fate.

2014-04-24.title: Turkey Offers Condolences to Armenians Over Killings
2014-04-24.abstract: Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s office posted a web statement, but the government stood by its position that the killings of at least 1.5 million Armenians in 1915 were not a genocide.

2014-04-25.title: If Assad Wins War, Challenge From His Own Sect May Follow
2014-04-25.abstract: Some loyalists of President Bashar al-Assad of Syria would be likely to push for a bigger share of power if they came to feel safe from the threat of insurgent revenge.

2014-04-25.title: 2 Journalists in Lebanon Are Charged by Tribunal
2014-04-25.abstract: The international tribunal dealing with the killing of a former Lebanese prime minister has charged two journalists with contempt of court and obstruction of justice.

2014-04-25.title: U.S. Confronts Consequences of Underestimating North Korean Leader
2014-04-25.abstract: As President Obama visits South Korea, he will be confronting the question of whether his strategy of “strategic patience” with the North and its leader, Kim Jong-un, is irrelevant.

2014-04-25.title: Gang Leader From Mexico Gets Life Term
2014-04-25.abstract: According to U.S. officials, Arturo Gallegos Castrellón, 35, ordered hundreds of killings as cartels battled over control of Ciudad Juárez.

2014-04-25.title: Kerry Rejects ‘Absurd’ Russian Claims
2014-04-25.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry denounced Russian actions in Ukraine but stopped short of announcing measures in response.

2014-04-26.title: Saving China’s Elephants
2014-04-26.abstract: Efforts to save China’s few hundred wild elephants appear to have been successful although visitors to the Yunnan Province Wild Elephant Valley Park are unlikely to see one.

2014-04-26.title: In Land That Values Ivory, Wild Elephants Find a Safe Haven
2014-04-26.abstract: Government protections are helping Asian elephant numbers rise, but forests are rapidly giving way to rubber and other cash crops.

2014-04-26.title: European Firms Seek to Minimize Russia Sanctions
2014-04-26.abstract: As businesses, particularly in the energy sector, campaign to maintain ties with Moscow, the world’s seven wealthiest nations said on Friday that they would impose additional sanctions on Russia resulting from its military action in Ukraine.

2014-04-26.title: Nigerian Movie Appears to Hit Nerve Over War
2014-04-26.abstract: “Half of a Yellow Sun” was scheduled to open in Nigeria on Friday, but the country’s film censorship board has refused to issue the movie a certificate, effectively banning it.

2014-04-26.title: U.S. Says It Built Digital Programs Abroad With an Eye to Politics
2014-04-26.abstract: The United States built Twitter-like programs in Afghanistan and Pakistan, but like a similar program in Cuba, they were shut down for lack of money, officials said.

2014-04-27.title: Scots Ponder: Should They Stay or Should They Go?
2014-04-27.abstract: Over the last six months the momentum has shifted toward independence, but some fear the fervor that it is creating on either side of the campaign.

2014-04-27.title: South Korean Prime Minister Offers to Resign Over Deadly Ferry Disaster
2014-04-27.abstract: Prime Minister Chung Hong-won, the No. 2 official in the South Korean government, offered “an apology to the people.”

2014-04-27.title: Mahmoud Abbas Shifts on Holocaust
2014-04-27.abstract: The president of the Palestinian Authority issued a formal statement calling the Holocaust “the most heinous crime to have occurred against humanity in the modern era.”

2014-04-27.title: Sanctions Revive Search for Secret Putin Fortune
2014-04-27.abstract: President Obama’s response to the Ukraine crisis, while derided by critics as slow and weak, has reinvigorated a 15-year global hunt for the Russian leader’s hidden wealth.

2014-04-27.title: South Korean Leader Accepts Resignation of Premier Over Ferry Disaster
2014-04-27.abstract: President Park Geun-hye accepted the resignation of Prime Minister Chung Hong-won but asked him to stay in his post until the government completed its rescue operations, an official said.

2014-04-28.title: U.S. Weighs Harder Line on Russia Than European Allies
2014-04-28.abstract: The White House has become entangled in a debate over how much emphasis to put on unity with European allies reluctant to take stronger economic actions against Moscow, while pro-Russian forces in Ukraine continue to defy demands.

2014-04-28.title: In Venezuela, Protesters Point to Their Scars
2014-04-28.abstract: President Nicolás Maduro, fearing efforts to overthrow him, said he held the opposition responsible for the violence that, by some accounts, had claimed more than 40 lives.

2014-04-28.title: North Korean Peace Group Insults South Korean Leader
2014-04-28.abstract: The North’s Committee for the Peaceful Reunification of Korea described Park Geun-hye in misogynic terms and called President Obama her “pimp.”

2014-04-28.title: India Election Digest: April 28
2014-04-28.abstract: A roundup of election-related news for Monday.

2014-04-28.title: Jet Hunt Focus Shifts From Air to Under Sea
2014-04-28.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Abbott of Australia said Monday that the search for debris from Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 would enter a “new phase,” focusing on a much larger area underwater.

2014-04-29.title: Senate Drops Bid to Report on Drone Use
2014-04-29.abstract: The provision was quietly stripped from an intelligence bill, highlighting continuing government resistance on these operations.

2014-04-29.title: Syria Announces Assad’s Bid for Re-election as War Rages
2014-04-29.abstract: The election scheduled for June 3 is widely seen as an attempt to enhance President Bashar al-Assad’s perceived legitimacy despite the civil war that has displaced millions.

2014-04-29.title: Lull Prompts Question: What Are Taliban Up To?
2014-04-29.abstract: Officials in Afghanistan are urgently speculating about the absence of Taliban attacks since Election Day, suggesting the militants are biding their time, paralyzed by infighting, or worried about bad publicity.

2014-04-29.title: Mayor of Kharkiv, Ukraine, Is Shot
2014-04-29.abstract: Ukrainian officials discussed the shooting of Gennady A. Kernes, the mayor of Kharkiv, who was shot in the back while running on Monday.

2014-04-29.title: One City Falls to Pro-Russian Militants; in Another, the Mayor Is Shot
2014-04-29.abstract: Taken together, the events on Monday in Ukraine pointed to the further enfeeblement of the interim government in Kiev.

2014-04-30.title: Official Slams Brazil’s Olympic Planning
2014-04-30.abstract: The International Olympic Committee’s vice president, John D. Coates, said Brazil’s preparations for the 2016 Olympic Games were “the worst” he has ever experienced.

2014-04-30.title: Preparations for Rio Olympics ‘the Worst,’ Committee Official Says
2014-04-30.abstract: Doubts are growing about the city’s ability to get ready for the 2016 Summer Olympics without international help, and officials say there is no Plan B.

2014-04-30.title: With Peace Talks Off, Netanyahu Looks at Unilateral Moves
2014-04-30.abstract: The Israeli prime minister has asked his cabinet to bring forward alternative approaches to the Palestinian conflict, which are likely to include annexing parts of the West Bank or withdrawing from some settlements and shoring up others.

2014-04-30.title: Amid Iraq’s Unrest, Maliki Campaigns as Strongman
2014-04-30.abstract: A strategy of showing toughness may help lift Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki, but his success was far from certain in the first national vote since the withdrawal of American forces.

2014-04-30.title: An Alarm Over the Incursion of Foreign Words
2014-04-30.abstract: While a language’s absorption of foreign words is often seen as a positive mark of a society’s cosmopolitanism, the reaction in China has been anything but positive – at least according to state media.
